_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Translate (e.g., verbatim, gists, and/or summaries) target voice material.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of transformer architecture, attention mechanisms, and the pre-training and fine-tuning paradigm as it applies to language models.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI AI domain. AI-K-012 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-S-011 (AI, cosine: 0.395), OP-S-004 (OP, cosine: 0.371).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.2/10 (raw 0.322). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [gists, material, summaries, target, translate, verbatim] vs WIDAI-only terms [applies, architecture, attention, fine, knowledge, language] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.831 (P=0.822, R=0.840). Balanced P/R — the 9-word DCWF element and 18-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (98.6%). "Translate (e.g., verbatim, gists, and/or summaries) target voice material." and "Knowledge of transformer architecture, attention mechanisms, and the pre-trainin" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.401 > STS 0.322 (gap +0.080). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 3.5%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [gists, material, summaries, target, translate, verbatim]. WIDAI terms: [applies, architecture, attention, fine, knowledge, language].
